steps.text: Address the precise procedures of a sampling technique when developing or validating sampling tools and techniques.
Indicate all physical sampling techniques or collection tools that are used.
Indicate whether the procedure is a field measurement process such as screening or a direct reading.
Document the process for selecting a location representative of the analyte(s) distribution in the local environment.
Prioritize parameters for optimization, weighting toward parameters that represent significant programmatic concerns.
Optimize each significant parameter, either serially or using multi-parameter optimization.
Test previously optimized parameters to ensure that they remain optimal.
Make necessary adjustments to previously optimized parameters.
Detail the conditions and parameters considered in optimization in the technique description.
Note discontinuities in optimization parameters in the technique description.
Modify, amend, or otherwise annotate the technique.
Consider factors other than cost/sample to select the applicable optimization state.
Report results as less than the LOD per sample area or volume.
Develop a security plan that includes the site, staff, storage, and transport of potentially hazardous materials.
Contact state or local regulatory agencies early to determine state or local requirements and available treatment and disposal options.
Incorporate additional requirements to ensure adequate and proper data quality.
Ship suspected contaminated samples or wastes as dangerous goods or select agents.
Package, label, and ship potentially hazardous materials according to appropriate ground or air regulations.
Store samples or wastes in a secure area protected from vermin and adverse weather conditions.
Separate hazardous and nonhazardous samples and wastes.
Prepare a written description of the technique.
Include numerical and descriptive specifications of the technique's operational limits and performance attributes in the description.
Describe sampling technique performance after experiencing minor changes in operating or environmental conditions.
Describe the applicable resource requirements, such as personnel, skills, and equipment, as well as the required measurements to be taken, such as site parameters and matrix parameters.
Describe safety concerns and procedures that should be addressed, including personal protective equipment and first aid as well as physical and chemical hazards.
Describe the QC/QA checks used.
Discuss technique development.
Discuss validation testing results, including comparison with reference materials and preparations, acceptance criteria and recommendations.
Discuss sampling technique changes that were made as a result of the validation studies.
Discuss formal acceptance/rejection of work.
Provide recommendations for future work.

preconditions.text: Discontinuities are revealed after a technique is applied over a period of time
No organisms are detected in a sample
The corresponding health and safety plan must be approved by the local Safety, Health and Environmental Management (SHEM) office or the chemical hygiene officer before starting a project.
A management system should be in place before sampling or analytical work begins.

constraints.text: Address personal protective equipment and first aid in both the sampling technique and the validation plan.
Address physical and biological hazards such as harmful microorganisms.
Ensure that the properties of the control(s) are similar to those of the target organisms especially with regard to sampling when selecting surrogates.
Consider the ability to capture the surrogate in a manner similar to the target when selecting a sampling technique.
Maintain the integrity of target microorganism(s) until samples are analyzed.
Adhere to Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), International Air Transport Association (IATA), state, and other hazardous material shipping regulations.
Identify locations and contact information for local hospitals, emergency contacts, emergency showers, eyewash stations, and other first aid.
All hazardous goods shipments must comply with regulations and guidance.
Personnel with approved DOT/IATA training must perform all shipments of potentially hazardous materials.
Sample and packaging integrity must be maintained to ensure safe shipment.
Shipments may be subject to the Select Agent Rules (42 CFR 72.6).
The EMMC format is a recommended format and not a requirement.
